projectSummary: Project Phoenix has three critical deliverables landing this week, owned by Sarah's Development Team: John is completing API integration by Wednesday, Mary is delivering frontend components for testing by Thursday, and Alex is deploying database migrations by Friday. Sarah has flagged three risks that could derail the week — third-party API instability, an unprepared testing environment, and pending client approval on the UI designs — and asked the team to report status at tomorrow's standup.
objectives: Complete API integration by Wednesday so downstream services and frontend work can rely on a stable contract.
Ship frontend components in a testable state by Thursday so QA can begin Mary-driven UI validation.
Deploy database migrations to production by Friday with no data-loss regressions.
Surface and resolve three flagged risks — third-party API instability, test environment readiness, and pending client UI approval — before they block the weekly deliverables.

tasks.name: Deliver frontend components ready for testing

risks.name: Third-party API instability threatens John's Wednesday API integration milestone
risks.description: Third-party API instability threatens John's Wednesday API integration milestone
risks.solution: Pair John with the third-party API owner to add retries, timeouts and a circuit-breaker on the integration; build a mock/stub of the third-party API so integration work and testing can proceed even while the upstream is unstable; escalate to the API vendor for an ETA on stability before Wednesday's deliverable.

risks.name: Testing environment is not ready ahead of Mary's Thursday frontend testing milestone
risks.description: Testing environment is not ready ahead of Mary's Thursday frontend testing milestone
risks.solution: Get a clear owner on the test environment today; verify provision of test accounts, seed data, CI pipeline and access credentials before Mary's Thursday hand-off; if the environment will not be ready in time, define a minimum-testable cut of the frontend that Mary's team can validate locally.

risks.name: Client approval on UI designs is still pending and could block Mary's frontend work
risks.description: Client approval on UI designs is still pending and could block Mary's frontend work
risks.solution: Sarah to chase the client reviewer for a decision today; in parallel, Mary should build against the proposed designs and abstract client-specific copy/imagery so only the approved layer needs to be swapped in once feedback arrives.

recommendations.title: Treat tomorrow's standup as a hard synchronization point: each owner (John, Mary, Alex) reports status and any blocker before the standup closes so Sarah can reallocate mid-week if needed.
recommendations.description: Treat tomorrow's standup as a hard synchronization point: each owner (John, Mary, Alex) reports status and any blocker before the standup closes so Sarah can reallocate mid-week if needed.

recommendations.title: Escalate the third-party API issue today — it directly blocks John's Wednesday deadline, so a stability ETA or a working stub is needed within 24 hours.
recommendations.description: Escalate the third-party API issue today — it directly blocks John's Wednesday deadline, so a stability ETA or a working stub is needed within 24 hours.

recommendations.title: Make environment readiness a daily checklist for Mary's team, not a one-time ask, until the Thursday testing hand-off is verified end-to-end.
recommendations.description: Make environment readiness a daily checklist for Mary's team, not a one-time ask, until the Thursday testing hand-off is verified end-to-end.

recommendations.title: Sequence Alex's migration deployment after John and Mary land, so any schema changes match the API contract and the frontend's data expectations — roll back into a Friday-night window with a tested revert path.
recommendations.description: Sequence Alex's migration deployment after John and Mary land, so any schema changes match the API contract and the frontend's data expectations — roll back into a Friday-night window with a tested revert path.

recommendations.title: Push the client for an explicit UI-approval decision (or a written risk-acceptance) so Mary's work does not have to be rebuilt late in the cycle.
recommendations.description: Push the client for an explicit UI-approval decision (or a written risk-acceptance) so Mary's work does not have to be rebuilt late in the cycle.
